title: Oppervlaktecalculator — Rechthoek, Cirkel, Driehoek & Meer
description: Bereken de oppervlakte van elke vorm: rechthoek, vierkant, cirkel, driehoek, trapezium en meer. Directe resultaten met formule-uitleg.

faq.title: Veelgestelde Vragen
faq.q1: Hoe wordt de oppervlakte van een driehoek berekend?
faq.a1: De oppervlakte van een driehoek is gelijk aan de helft van de basis maal de hoogte: Oppervlakte = 0,5 × basis × hoogte. De hoogte moet loodrecht op de basis staan. Voorbeeld: driehoek met basis 6 en hoogte 8: Oppervlakte = 0,5 × 6 × 8 = 24 vierkante eenheden.
faq.q2: Wat is π en waarom verschijnt het in cirkelberekeningen?
faq.a2: Pi (π ≈ 3,14159) is de verhouding van de omtrek van een cirkel tot zijn diameter. Het verschijnt in de cirkeloppervlakte (π × r²) omdat de oppervlakte van een cirkel kan worden afgeleid door hem uit te rollen tot een driehoek met basis = omtrek (2πr) en hoogte = straal (r).
faq.q3: Wat is het verschil tussen oppervlakte en omtrek?
faq.a3: Oppervlakte meet de tweedimensionale ruimte binnen een vorm — uitgedrukt in vierkante eenheden (cm², m²). Omtrek meet de totale afstand rond de grens van een vorm — uitgedrukt in lineaire eenheden (cm, m). Voor een kamer van 4 m × 3 m: oppervlakte = 12 m², omtrek = 14 m.
faq.q4: Hoe wordt de oppervlakte van een trapezium berekend?
faq.a4: Een trapezium heeft twee parallelle zijden. De oppervlakte is het gemiddelde van de twee parallelle zijden maal de hoogte: Oppervlakte = 0,5 × (zijde1 + zijde2) × hoogte. Voorbeeld: trapezium met parallelle zijden 6 m en 10 m, hoogte 4 m: Oppervlakte = 0,5 × 16 × 4 = 32 m².
faq.q5: Waarom is de omtrekformule voor een ellips een benadering?
faq.a5: In tegenstelling tot de oppervlakte (π × a × b) heeft de omtrek van een ellips geen exacte gesloten formule. Deze calculator gebruikt de benadering van Ramanujan: P ≈ π × [3(a+b) − √((3a+b)(a+3b))], die nauwkeurig is tot binnen 0,0002% voor de meeste ellipsen.
faq.q6: Hoe bereken ik de oppervlakte van een onregelmatige ruimte?
faq.a6: Splits onregelmatige vormen op in standaardvormen en tel de oppervlakten op. Een L-vormige kamer kan worden gesplitst in twee rechthoeken. Voor onregelmatige percelen met bekende coördinaten geeft de schoenveterformule exacte resultaten.
faq.q7: Hoeveel verf of vloerbedekking heb ik nodig?
faq.a7: Bereken eerst de oppervlakte. Standaard muurverf dekt 10–12 m² per liter. Laminaat of houten vloer: tel 10% extra op voor snijverlies. Tegels: tel 10–15% extra op. Voor muren: meet elke muur (lengte × hoogte), trek ramen en deuren af, tel alle muren op.
faq.q8: Welke eenheden worden gebruikt voor oppervlaktemeting?
faq.a8: Veelgebruikte eenheden: cm² (kleine objecten), m² (kamers, vloeren), km² (geografische gebieden), hectare = 10.000 m² (landbouw), acre = 4.047 m² (VS), ft² (VS vastgoed). Omrekening: 1 m² = 10.000 cm² = 10,764 ft².

content.whatIs:
The area calculator computes the area and perimeter of six common geometric shapes — rectangle, square, circle, triangle, trapezoid, and ellipse — using the standard mathematical formulas for each shape. Select your shape, enter the required dimensions, and instantly receive both the enclosed area (in square units) and the boundary perimeter (in linear units).

Area and perimeter calculations are among the most universally applicable geometric computations in everyday life. Every home improvement project — flooring, tiling, painting, fencing, gardening — requires knowing the area of a surface or the perimeter of a boundary. Architects and interior designers calculate room areas to determine material quantities. Real estate listings specify floor areas in square feet or square meters as the primary measurement of home size. Agricultural land is measured in acres or hectares — both derived from area calculations. Engineers calculate cross-sectional areas of structural members to determine load-bearing capacity.

This calculator covers the six most commonly needed geometric shapes, providing both the formula and the numerical result for each. Understanding the underlying formulas — not just the results — allows you to decompose more complex irregular shapes into these basic building blocks and sum the components. A floor plan that is L-shaped is two rectangles; a circular garden pond is a circle subtracted from a square; a trapezoidal room is exactly what the trapezoid formula handles directly.
content.formula:
Rectangle:
  Area = a × b
  Perimeter = 2 × (a + b)

Square:
  Area = a²
  Perimeter = 4 × a

Circle:
  Area = π × r²
  Perimeter (circumference) = 2 × π × r
  (where r = radius = a input)

Triangle (base and height):
  Area = 0.5 × a × b
  (a = base, b = perpendicular height)
  Perimeter requires all 3 sides; right triangle: hypotenuse = √(a² + b²)

Trapezoid:
  Area = 0.5 × (a + b) × h
  (a, b = parallel sides, h = perpendicular height)

Ellipse:
  Area = π × a × b
  (a = semi-major axis, b = semi-minor axis)
  Perimeter ≈ π × [3(a+b) − √((3a+b)(a+3b))]  (Ramanujan approximation)
content.example:
Example 1 — Room flooring (rectangle):
Room: 5.2 m × 4.8 m
Area = 5.2 × 4.8 = 24.96 m² → need 24.96 m² of flooring
Perimeter = 2 × (5.2 + 4.8) = 20 m → need 20 m of baseboard

Example 2 — Garden pond (circle):
Pond radius: 2.5 m
Area = π × 2.5² = π × 6.25 ≈ 19.63 m²
Circumference = 2π × 2.5 ≈ 15.71 m → edging needed

Example 3 — Triangular plot:
Base: 12 m, perpendicular height: 9 m
Area = 0.5 × 12 × 9 = 54 m²

Example 4 — Oval flowerbed (ellipse):
Semi-major axis: 4 m, semi-minor axis: 2.5 m
Area = π × 4 × 2.5 ≈ 31.42 m²
Perimeter ≈ π × [3(6.5) − √(14.5 × 6.5)] ≈ 21.4 m

Example 5 — Trapezoid driveway:
Parallel sides: 6 m and 9 m, height: 12 m
Area = 0.5 × (6 + 9) × 12 = 90 m²
content.howItWorks_1: Select your shape from the dropdown menu. The input labels update to show which dimensions are needed for that shape. For rectangle: length and width. For square: side length (only one input needed). For circle: radius. For triangle: base length and perpendicular height. For trapezoid: the two parallel sides (a and b) and the perpendicular height between them. For ellipse: the semi-major axis (longer half) and semi-minor axis (shorter half).
content.howItWorks_2: Enter the dimensions in your preferred unit. The calculator does not specify units — if you enter dimensions in meters, the area will be in m² and perimeter in meters. If you enter in feet, outputs are in ft² and feet. Ensure all inputs use the same unit for consistent results.
content.howItWorks_3: Click Calculate to see the area in square units and the perimeter in linear units for your shape. Use the area to calculate materials needed for surface coverage. Use the perimeter to calculate edge materials (fencing, baseboard, edging). For real-world projects, add 10–15% to the area calculation to account for cuts, waste, and pattern matching.
content.whyItMatters:
Area calculation is the bridge between geometric theory and practical decision-making in construction, real estate, agriculture, and manufacturing. In home improvement alone, accurate area calculation prevents both expensive material waste and embarrassing shortfalls. Overestimating by 20% means buying and paying for €400 of unnecessary flooring. Underestimating by 10% means a second store trip mid-project and potentially mismatched dye lots. The few minutes spent accurately calculating room area pays back immediately in material cost savings.

In real estate, floor area is the primary metric used to value, compare, and regulate residential and commercial property. A miscalculation of 5 m² in a property listing can misrepresent a home's value by tens of thousands of dollars in high-value markets. Many countries regulate how floor area is measured for legal purposes — whether to include hallways, closets, stairwells, or external walls — making precise area calculation a legally relevant calculation in property transactions.

In agriculture, area calculation is directly tied to resource planning and financial projection. A 10-hectare field requires specific quantities of seed, fertilizer, and pesticide per hectare — errors in area estimation translate directly into over- or under-application of inputs, affecting yield and profitability. Land valuation for purchase or lease is denominated in price per acre or hectare, making area the fundamental metric for agricultural land economics. GPS-based field measurement tools are widely used precisely because area miscalculation at field scale is financially consequential.
content.limitations:
This calculator computes area and perimeter using exact or well-established approximate formulas for six standard shapes. It does not handle irregular shapes with non-standard boundaries — L-shapes, U-shapes, polygons with more than four sides, or curved boundaries other than circles and ellipses. For these, decompose the shape into standard components calculated separately, then sum the areas.

For triangle calculations, this calculator uses the base and perpendicular height formula (Area = 0.5 × base × height). If you know only the three side lengths but not the height, use Heron's formula: s = (a+b+c)/2; Area = √(s × (s-a) × (s-b) × (s-c)). If you know two sides and the included angle, Area = 0.5 × a × b × sin(C). These alternative triangle formulas are not implemented in this calculator but can be computed with a scientific calculator.

The perimeter formula for the triangle shown here assumes a right triangle (using the Pythagorean theorem for the hypotenuse). For non-right triangles where you know base, height, but not all three side lengths, the perimeter cannot be calculated from just two inputs. In that case, the perimeter output may not apply to your specific triangle geometry. For accurate non-right triangle perimeter, you need all three side lengths.
content.tips_1: For flooring, always add 10% to your calculated area for cuts and waste. For patterns or diagonal installations, add 15%. For small rooms with many cut edges (bathrooms, L-shapes), add up to 20%. This buffer prevents the situation of being short by one or two tiles or planks and needing to order a new batch — which may not match the existing dye lot.
content.tips_2: For paint calculations, remember that you typically need two coats and must account for both walls and ceiling separately. Measure each wall individually (height × width), subtract door and window areas, sum all walls, then add ceiling area (= floor area for flat ceilings). Divide total area by paint coverage per liter (typically 10 m²/liter for standard latex) and multiply by 2 for two coats. Round up to the nearest can size.
content.tips_3: Convert between area units confidently: 1 m² = 10.764 ft². To find how many m² a property described in ft² represents: divide by 10.764. A 2,000 ft² home = 2,000/10.764 ≈ 185.8 m². For agricultural land: 1 acre = 4,047 m² ≈ 0.405 hectares. 1 hectare = 10,000 m² = 2.471 acres. Knowing these conversions allows you to compare international property listings and agricultural specifications without confusion.
content.tips_4: For circular areas (garden beds, pools, driveways with curved ends), remember that radius is half the diameter. If you measure across the middle of a circular pond and get 5 meters, the radius is 2.5 meters. Area = π × 2.5² ≈ 19.6 m². A common error is entering the diameter as the radius, which quadruples the calculated area. Always verify: is the measurement from center to edge (radius) or from edge to edge (diameter)?
